item.title: SEAR: Schema-Based Evaluation and Routing for LLM Gateways
item.description:
By Zecheng Zhang, Han Zheng, Yue Xu
Evaluating production LLM responses and routing requests across providers in LLM gateways requires fine-grained quality signals and operationally grounded decisions. To address this gap, we present SEAR, a schema-based evaluation and routing system for multi-model, multi-provider LLM gateways. SEAR ...

item.title: Sommelier: Scalable Open Multi-turn Audio Pre-processing for Full-duplex Speech Language Models
item.description:
By Kyudan Jung, Jihwan Kim, Soyoon Kim, Jeongoon Kim, Jaegul Choo, et al.
As the paradigm of AI shifts from text-based LLMs to Speech Language Models (SLMs), there is a growing demand for full-duplex systems capable of real-time, natural human-computer interaction. However, the development of such models is constrained by the scarcity of high-quality, multi-speaker conver...

item.title: Diffutron: A Masked Diffusion Language Model for Turkish Language
item.description:
By Şuayp Talha Kocabay, Talha Rüzgar Akkuş
Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) have emerged as a compelling non-autoregressive alternative to standard large language models; however, their application to morphologically rich languages remains limited. In this paper, we introduce Diffutron, a masked diffusion language model specifically ...

item.title: Lie to Me: How Faithful Is Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Reasoning Models?
item.description:
By Richard J. Young
Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning has been proposed as a transparency mechanism for large language models in safety-critical deployments, yet its effectiveness depends on faithfulness (whether models accurately verbalize the factors that actually influence their outputs), a property that prior evalua...

item.title: Know3D: Prompting 3D Generation with Knowledge from Vision-Language Models
item.description:
By Wenyue Chen, Wenjue Chen, Peng Li, Qinghe Wang, Xu Jia, et al.
Recent advances in 3D generation have improved the fidelity and geometric details of synthesized 3D assets. However, due to the inherent ambiguity of single-view observations and the lack of robust global structural priors caused by limited 3D training data, the unseen regions generated by existing ...

item.title: LongTail Driving Scenarios with Reasoning Traces: The KITScenes LongTail Dataset
item.description:
By Royden Wagner, Omer Sahin Tas, Jaime Villa, Felix Hauser, Yinzhe Shen, et al.
In real-world domains such as self-driving, generalization to rare scenarios remains a fundamental challenge. To address this, we introduce a new dataset designed for end-to-end driving that focuses on long-tail driving events. We provide multi-view video data, trajectories, high-level instructions,...

item.title: GenMask: Adapting DiT for Segmentation via Direct Mask
item.description:
By Yuhuan Yang, Xianwei Zhuang, Yuxuan Cai, Chaofan Ma, Shuai Bai, et al.
Recent approaches for segmentation have leveraged pretrained generative models as feature extractors, treating segmentation as a downstream adaptation task via indirect feature retrieval. This implicit use suffers from a fundamental misalignment in representation. It also depends heavily on indirect...

item.title: Composer 2 Technical Report
item.description:
By Cursor Research, Aaron Chan, Ahmed Shalaby, Alexander Wettig, Aman Sanger, et al.
Composer 2 is a specialized model designed for agentic software engineering. The model demonstrates strong long-term planning and coding intelligence while maintaining the ability to efficiently solve problems for interactive use. The model is trained in two phases: first, continued pretraining to i...

item.title: AVO: Agentic Variation Operators for Autonomous Evolutionary Search
item.description:
By Terry Chen, Zhifan Ye, Bing Xu, Zihao Ye, Timmy Liu, et al.
Agentic Variation Operators (AVO) are a new family of evolutionary variation operators that replace the fixed mutation, crossover, and hand-designed heuristics of classical evolutionary search with autonomous coding agents. Rather than confining a language model to candidate generation within a pres...

item.title: MedOpenClaw: Auditable Medical Imaging Agents Reasoning over Uncurated Full Studies
item.description:
By Weixiang Shen, Yanzhu Hu, Che Liu, Junde Wu, Jiayuan Zhu, et al.
Currently, evaluating vision-language models (VLMs) in medical imaging tasks oversimplifies clinical reality by relying on pre-selected 2D images that demand significant manual labor to curate. This setup misses the core challenge of realworld diagnostics: a true clinical agent must actively navigat...

item.title: VFIG: Vectorizing Complex Figures in SVG with Vision-Language Models
item.description:
By Qijia He, Xunmei Liu, Hammaad Memon, Ziang Li, Zixian Ma, et al.
Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) are an essential format for technical illustration and digital design, offering precise resolution independence and flexible semantic editability. In practice, however, original vector source files are frequently lost or inaccessible, leaving only "flat" rasterized ver...

item.title: WAFT-Stereo: Warping-Alone Field Transforms for Stereo Matching
item.description:
By Yihan Wang, Jia Deng
We introduce WAFT-Stereo, a simple and effective warping-based method for stereo matching. WAFT-Stereo demonstrates that cost volumes, a common design used in many leading methods, are not necessary for strong performance and can be replaced by warping with improved efficiency. WAFT-Stereo ranks fir...

item.title: Reaching Beyond the Mode: RL for Distributional Reasoning in Language Models
item.description:
By Isha Puri, Mehul Damani, Idan Shenfeld, Marzyeh Ghassemi, Jacob Andreas, et al.
Given a question, a language model (LM) implicitly encodes a distribution over possible answers. In practice, post-training procedures for LMs often collapse this distribution onto a single dominant mode. While this is generally not a problem for benchmark-style evaluations that assume one correct a...

item.title: Can MLLMs Read Students' Minds? Unpacking Multimodal Error Analysis in Handwritten Math
item.description:
By Dingjie Song, Tianlong Xu, Yi-Fan Zhang, Hang Li, Zhiling Yan, et al.
Assessing student handwritten scratchwork is crucial for personalized educational feedback but presents unique challenges due to diverse handwriting, complex layouts, and varied problem-solving approaches. Existing educational NLP primarily focuses on textual responses and neglects the complexity an...

item.title: Trace2Skill: Distill Trajectory-Local Lessons into Transferable Agent Skills
item.description:
By Jingwei Ni, Yihao Liu, Xinpeng Liu, Yutao Sun, Mengyu Zhou, et al.
Equipping Large Language Model (LLM) agents with domain-specific skills is critical for tackling complex tasks. Yet, manual authoring creates a severe scalability bottleneck. Conversely, automated skill generation often yields fragile or fragmented results because it either relies on shallow paramet...

item.title: Out of Sight but Not Out of Mind: Hybrid Memory for Dynamic Video World Models
item.description:
By Kaijin Chen, Dingkang Liang, Xin Zhou, Yikang Ding, Xiaoqiang Liu, et al.
Video world models have shown immense potential in simulating the physical world, yet existing memory mechanisms primarily treat environments as static canvases. When dynamic subjects hide out of sight and later re-emerge, current methods often struggle, leading to frozen, distorted, or vanishing su...

item.title: Natural-Language Agent Harnesses
item.description:
By Linyue Pan, Lexiao Zou, Shuo Guo, Jingchen Ni, Hai-Tao Zheng
Agent performance increasingly depends on harness engineering, yet harness design is usually buried in controller code and runtime-specific conventions, making it hard to transfer, compare, and study as a scientific object. We ask whether the high-level control logic of an agent harness can instead ...

item.title: PackForcing: Short Video Training Suffices for Long Video Sampling and Long Context Inference
item.description:
By Xiaofeng Mao, Shaohao Rui, Kaining Ying, Bo Zheng, Chuanhao Li, et al.
Autoregressive video diffusion models have demonstrated remarkable progress, yet they remain bottlenecked by intractable linear KV-cache growth, temporal repetition, and compounding errors during long-video generation. To address these challenges, we present PackForcing, a unified framework that eff...

item.title: ShotStream: Streaming Multi-Shot Video Generation for Interactive Storytelling
item.description:
By Yawen Luo, Xiaoyu Shi, Junhao Zhuang, Yutian Chen, Quande Liu, et al.
Multi-shot video generation is crucial for long narrative storytelling, yet current bidirectional architectures suffer from limited interactivity and high latency. We propose ShotStream, a novel causal multi-shot architecture that enables interactive storytelling and efficient on-the-fly frame gener...

item.title: RealChart2Code: Advancing Chart-to-Code Generation with Real Data and Multi-Task Evaluation
item.description:
By Jiajun Zhang, Yuying Li, Zhixun Li, Xingyu Guo, Jingzhuo Wu, et al.
Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities in code generation across various domains. However, their ability to replicate complex, multi-panel visualizations from real-world data remains largely unassessed. To address this gap, we introduce \texttt{RealChart2Code}, a new...

item.title: Density-aware Soft Context Compression with Semi-Dynamic Compression Ratio
item.description:
By Yijiong Yu, Shuai Yuan, Jie Zheng, Huazheng Wang, Ji Pei
Soft context compression reduces the computational workload of processing long contexts in LLMs by encoding long context into a smaller number of latent tokens. However, existing frameworks apply uniform compression ratios, failing to account for the extreme variance in natural language information ...

item.title: Think over Trajectories: Leveraging Video Generation to Reconstruct GPS Trajectories from Cellular Signaling
item.description:
By Ruixing Zhang, Hanzhang Jiang, Leilei Sun, Liangzhe Han, Jibin Wang, et al.
Mobile devices continuously interact with cellular base stations, generating massive volumes of signaling records that provide broad coverage for understanding human mobility. However, such records offer only coarse location cues (e.g., serving-cell identifiers) and therefore limit their direct use ...

item.title: Make Geometry Matter for Spatial Reasoning
item.description:
By Shihua Zhang, Qiuhong Shen, Shizun Wang, Tianbo Pan, Xinchao Wang
Empowered by large-scale training, vision-language models (VLMs) achieve strong image and video understanding, yet their ability to perform spatial reasoning in both static scenes and dynamic videos remains limited. Recent advances try to handle this limitation by injecting geometry tokens from pret...

item.title: Learning to Commit: Generating Organic Pull Requests via Online Repository Memory
item.description:
By Mo Li, L. H. Xu, Qitai Tan, Ting Cao, Yunxin Liu
Large language model (LLM)-based coding agents achieve impressive results on controlled benchmarks yet routinely produce pull requests that real maintainers reject. The root cause is not functional incorrectness but a lack of organicity: generated code ignores project-specific conventions, duplicate...

item.title: TAPS: Task Aware Proposal Distributions for Speculative Sampling
item.description:
By Mohamad Zbib, Mohamad Bazzi, Ammar Mohanna, Hasan Abed Al Kader Hammoud, Bernard Ghanem
Speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive generation by letting a lightweight draft model propose future tokens that a larger target model then verifies in parallel. In practice, however, draft models are usually trained on broad generic corpora, which leaves it unclear how much speculative de...

item.title: Unified Number-Free Text-to-Motion Generation Via Flow Matching
item.description:
By Guanhe Huang, Oya Celiktutan
Generative models excel at motion synthesis for a fixed number of agents but struggle to generalize with variable agents. Based on limited, domain-specific data, existing methods employ autoregressive models to generate motion recursively, which suffer from inefficiency and error accumulation. We pr...

item.title: MOOZY: A Patient-First Foundation Model for Computational Pathology
item.description:
By Yousef Kotp, Vincent Quoc-Huy Trinh, Christopher Pal, Mahdi S. Hosseini
Computational pathology needs whole-slide image (WSI) foundation models that transfer across diverse clinical tasks, yet current approaches remain largely slide-centric, often depend on private data and expensive paired-report supervision, and do not explicitly model relationships among multiple sli...

item.title: Text Data Integration
item.description:
By Md Ataur Rahman, Dimitris Sacharidis, Oscar Romero, Sergi Nadal
Data comes in many forms. From a shallow perspective, they can be viewed as being either in structured (e.g., as a relation, as key-value pairs) or unstructured (e.g., text, image) formats. So far, machines have been fairly good at processing and reasoning over structured data that follows a precise...

item.title: Story2Proposal: A Scaffold for Structured Scientific Paper Writing
item.description:
By Zhuoyang Qian, Wei Shi, Xu Lin, Li Ling, Meng Luo, et al.
Generating scientific manuscripts requires maintaining alignment between narrative reasoning, experimental evidence, and visual artifacts across the document lifecycle. Existing language-model generation pipelines rely on unconstrained text synthesis with validation applied only after generation, of...

item.title: EpochX: Building the Infrastructure for an Emergent Agent Civilization
item.description:
By Huacan Wang, Chaofa Yuan, Xialie Zhuang, Tu Hu, Shuo Zhang, et al.
General-purpose technologies reshape economies less by improving individual tools than by enabling new ways to organize production and coordination. We believe AI agents are approaching a similar inflection point: as foundation models make broad task execution and tool use increasingly accessible, t...

item.title: A Comparative Study in Surgical AI: Datasets, Foundation Models, and Barriers to Med-AGI
item.description:
By Kirill Skobelev, Eric Fithian, Yegor Baranovski, Jack Cook, Sandeep Angara, et al.
Recent Artificial Intelligence (AI) models have matched or exceeded human experts in several benchmarks of biomedical task performance, but have lagged behind on surgical image-analysis benchmarks. Since surgery requires integrating disparate tasks -- including multimodal data integration, human int...

item.title: On Token's Dilemma: Dynamic MoE with Drift-Aware Token Assignment for Continual Learning of Large Vision Language Models
item.description:
By Chongyang Zhao, Mingsong Li, Haodong Lu, Dong Gong
Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning aims to continually enhance Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) by learning from new data without forgetting previously acquired knowledge. Mixture of Experts (MoE) architectures naturally facilitate this by incrementally adding new experts and expanding rout...

item.title: PRBench: End-to-end Paper Reproduction in Physics Research
item.description:
By Shi Qiu, Junyi Deng, Yiwei Deng, Haoran Dong, Jieyu Fu, et al.
AI agents powered by large language models exhibit strong reasoning and problem-solving capabilities, enabling them to assist scientific research tasks such as formula derivation and code generation. However, whether these agents can reliably perform end-to-end reproduction from real scientific pape...

item.title: KAT-Coder-V2 Technical Report
item.description:
By Fengxiang Li, Han Zhang, Haoyang Huang, Jinghui Wang, Jinhua Hao, et al.
We present KAT-Coder-V2, an agentic coding model developed by the KwaiKAT team at Kuaishou. KAT-Coder-V2 adopts a "Specialize-then-Unify" paradigm that decomposes agentic coding into five expert domains - SWE, WebCoding, Terminal, WebSearch, and General - each undergoing independent supervised fine-...

item.title: Emergent Social Intelligence Risks in Generative Multi-Agent Systems
item.description:
By Yue Huang, Yu Jiang, Wenjie Wang, Haomin Zhuang, Xiaonan Luo, et al.
Multi-agent systems composed of large generative models are rapidly moving from laboratory prototypes to real-world deployments, where they jointly plan, negotiate, and allocate shared resources to solve complex tasks. While such systems promise unprecedented scalability and autonomy, their collecti...

item.title: MuSEAgent: A Multimodal Reasoning Agent with Stateful Experiences
item.description:
By Shijian Wang, Jiarui Jin, Runhao Fu, Zexuan Yan, Xingjian Wang, et al.
Research agents have recently achieved significant progress in information seeking and synthesis across heterogeneous textual and visual sources. In this paper, we introduce MuSEAgent, a multimodal reasoning agent that enhances decision-making by extending the capabilities of research agents to disc...

item.title: ImagenWorld: Stress-Testing Image Generation Models with Explainable Human Evaluation on Open-ended Real-World Tasks
item.description:
By Samin Mahdizadeh Sani, Max Ku, Nima Jamali, Matina Mahdizadeh Sani, Paria Khoshtab, et al.
Advances in diffusion, autoregressive, and hybrid models have enabled high-quality image synthesis for tasks such as text-to-image, editing, and reference-guided composition. Yet, existing benchmarks remain limited, either focus on isolated tasks, cover only narrow domains, or provide opaque scores ...

item.title: MolmoPoint: Better Pointing for VLMs with Grounding Tokens
item.description:
By Christopher Clark, Yue Yang, Jae Sung Park, Zixian Ma, Jieyu Zhang, et al.
Grounding has become a fundamental capability of vision-language models (VLMs). Most existing VLMs point by generating coordinates as part of their text output, which requires learning a complicated coordinate system and results in a high token count. Instead, we propose a more intuitive pointing me...

item.title: Kernel-Smith: A Unified Recipe for Evolutionary Kernel Optimization
item.description:
By He Du, Qiming Ge, Jiakai Hu, Aijun Yang, Zheng Cai, et al.
We present Kernel-Smith, a framework for high-performance GPU kernel and operator generation that combines a stable evaluation-driven evolutionary agent with an evolution-oriented post-training recipe. On the agent side, Kernel-Smith maintains a population of executable candidates and iteratively im...

item.title: Marco DeepResearch: Unlocking Efficient Deep Research Agents via Verification-Centric Design
item.description:
By Bin Zhu, Qianghuai Jia, Tian Lan, Junyang Ren, Feng Gu, et al.
Deep research agents autonomously conduct open-ended investigations, integrating complex information retrieval with multi-step reasoning across diverse sources to solve real-world problems. To sustain this capability on long-horizon tasks, reliable verification is critical during both training and i...

item.title: HISA: Efficient Hierarchical Indexing for Fine-Grained Sparse Attention
item.description:
By Yufei Xu, Fanxu Meng, Fan Jiang, Yuxuan Wang, Ruijie Zhou, et al.
Token-level sparse attention mechanisms, exemplified by DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA), achieve fine-grained key selection by scoring every historical token for each query using a lightweight indexer, and then computing attention only over the selected subset. While the downstream sparse attention ...

item.title: GEditBench v2: A Human-Aligned Benchmark for General Image Editing
item.description:
By Zhangqi Jiang, Zheng Sun, Xianfang Zeng, Yufeng Yang, Xuanyang Zhang, et al.
Recent advances in image editing have enabled models to handle complex instructions with impressive realism. However, existing evaluation frameworks lag behind: current benchmarks suffer from narrow task coverage, while standard metrics fail to adequately capture visual consistency, i.e., the preser...

item.title: Towards a Medical AI Scientist
item.description:
By Hongtao Wu, Boyun Zheng, Dingjie Song, Yu Jiang, Jianfeng Gao, et al.
Autonomous systems that generate scientific hypotheses, conduct experiments, and draft manuscripts have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for accelerating discovery. However, existing AI Scientists remain largely domain-agnostic, limiting their applicability to clinical medicine, where resear...

item.title: ResAdapt: Adaptive Resolution for Efficient Multimodal Reasoning
item.description:
By Huanxuan Liao, Zhongtao Jiang, Yupu Hao, Yuqiao Tan, Shizhu He, et al.
Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve stronger visual understanding by scaling input fidelity, yet the resulting visual token growth makes jointly sustaining high spatial resolution and long temporal context prohibitive. We argue that the bottleneck lies not in how post-encoding represent...

item.title: Superintelligence and Law
item.description:
By Noam Kolt
The prospect of artificial superintelligence -- AI agents that can generally outperform humans in cognitive tasks and economically valuable activities -- will transform the legal order as we know it. Operating autonomously or under only limited human oversight, AI agents will assume a growing range ...

item.title: AdaptToken: Entropy-based Adaptive Token Selection for MLLM Long Video Understanding
item.description:
By Haozhe Qi, Kevin Qu, Mahdi Rad, Rui Wang, Alexander Mathis, et al.
Long video understanding remains challenging for Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) due to high memory costs and context-length limits. Prior approaches mitigate this by scoring and selecting frames/tokens within short clips, but they lack a principled mechanism to (i) compare relevance acros...

item.title: DreamLite: A Lightweight On-Device Unified Model for Image Generation and Editing
item.description:
By Kailai Feng, Yuxiang Wei, Bo Chen, Yang Pan, Hu Ye, et al.
Diffusion models have made significant progress in both text-to-image (T2I) generation and text-guided image editing. However, these models are typically built with billions of parameters, leading to high latency and increased deployment challenges. While on-device diffusion models improve efficienc...

item.title: On-the-fly Repulsion in the Contextual Space for Rich Diversity in Diffusion Transformers
item.description:
By Omer Dahary, Benaya Koren, Daniel Garibi, Daniel Cohen-Or
Modern Text-to-Image (T2I) diffusion models have achieved remarkable semantic alignment, yet they often suffer from a significant lack of variety, converging on a narrow set of visual solutions for any given prompt. This typicality bias presents a challenge for creative applications that require a w...

item.title: HandX: Scaling Bimanual Motion and Interaction Generation
item.description:
By Zimu Zhang, Yucheng Zhang, Xiyan Xu, Ziyin Wang, Sirui Xu, et al.
Synthesizing human motion has advanced rapidly, yet realistic hand motion and bimanual interaction remain underexplored. Whole-body models often miss the fine-grained cues that drive dexterous behavior, finger articulation, contact timing, and inter-hand coordination, and existing resources lack hig...

item.title: Gen-Searcher: Reinforcing Agentic Search for Image Generation
item.description:
By Kaituo Feng, Manyuan Zhang, Shuang Chen, Yunlong Lin, Kaixuan Fan, et al.
Recent image generation models have shown strong capabilities in generating high-fidelity and photorealistic images. However, they are fundamentally constrained by frozen internal knowledge, thus often failing on real-world scenarios that are knowledge-intensive or require up-to-date information. In...
